name: markdown-it-py

metadata.name: markdown-it-py
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Python port of markdown-it. Markdown parsing, done right!

documentation:
# markdown-it-py [![Github-CI][github-ci]][github-link] [![Coverage Status][codecov-badge]][codecov-link] [![PyPI][pypi-badge]][pypi-link] [![Conda][conda-badge]][conda-link] [![Code style: black][black-badge]][black-link] [![PyPI - Downloads][install-badge]][install-link] > Markdown parser done right. - Follows the __[CommonMark spec](http://spec.commonmark.org/)__ for baseline parsing - Configurable syntax: you can add new rules and even replace existing ones. - Pluggable: Adds syntax extensions to extend the parser (see the [plugin list][md-plugins]). - High speed (see our [benchmarking tests][md-performance]) - [Safe by default][md-security] - Member of [Google's Assured Open Source Software](https://cloud.google.com/assured-open-source-software/docs/supported-packages) This is a Python port of [markdown-it], and some of its associated plugins. For more details see: . For details on [markdown-it] itself, see: - The __[Live demo](https://markdown-it.github.io)__ - [The markdown-it README][markdown-it-readme] ## Installation ```bash conda install -c conda-forge markdown-it-py ``` or ```bash pip install markdown-it-py[plugins] ``` or with extras ```bash conda install -c conda-forge markdown-it-py linkify-it-py mdit-py-plugins pip install markdown-it-py[linkify,plugins] ``` ## Usage ### Python API Usage Render markdown to HTML with markdown-it-py and a custom configuration with and without plugins and features: ```python from markdown_it import MarkdownIt from mdit_py_plugins.front_matter import front_matter_plugin from mdit_py_plugins.footnote import footnote_plugin md = ( MarkdownIt('commonmark' ,{'breaks':True,'html':True}) .use(front_matter_plugin) .use(footnote_plugin) .enable('table') ) text = (""" --- a: 1 --- a | b - | - 1 | 2 A footnote [^1] [^1]: some details """) tokens = md.parse(text) html_text = md.render(text) ## To export the html to a file, uncomment the lines below: # from pathlib import Path # Path("output.html").write_text(html_text) ``` ### Command-line Usage Render markdown to HTML with markdown-it-py from the command-line: ```console usage: markdown-it [-h] [-v] [filenames [filenames ...]] Parse one or more markdown files, convert each to HTML, and print to stdout positional arguments: filenames specify an optional list of files to convert optional arguments: -h, --help show this help message and exit -v, --version show program's version number and exit Interactive: $ markdown-it markdown-it-py [version 0.0.0] (interactive) Type Ctrl-D to complete input, or Ctrl-C to exit. >>> # Example ... > markdown *input* ...

Example

markdown input

Batch: $ markdown-it README.md README.footer.md > index.html ``` ## References / Thanks Big thanks to the authors of [markdown-it]: - Alex Kocharin [github/rlidwka](https://github.com/rlidwka) - Vitaly Puzrin [github/puzrin](https://github.com/puzrin) Also [John MacFarlane](https://github.com/jgm) for his work on the CommonMark spec and reference implementations. [github-ci]: https://github.com/executablebooks/markdown-it-py/workflows/Python%20package/badge.svg?branch=master [github-link]: https://github.com/executablebooks/markdown-it-py [pypi-badge]: https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/markdown-it-py.svg [pypi-link]: https://pypi.org/project/markdown-it-py [conda-badge]: https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/markdown-it-py/badges/version.svg [conda-link]: https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/markdown-it-py [codecov-badge]: https://codecov.io/gh/executablebooks/markdown-it-py/branch/master/graph/badge.svg [codecov-link]: https://codecov.io/gh/executablebooks/markdown-it-py [black-badge]: https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg [black-link]: https://github.com/ambv/black [install-badge]: https://img.shields.io/pypi/dw/markdown-it-py?label=pypi%20installs [install-link]: https://pypistats.org/packages/markdown-it-py [CommonMark spec]: http://spec.commonmark.org/ [markdown-it]: https://github.com/markdown-it/markdown-it [markdown-it-readme]: https://github.com/markdown-it/markdown-it/blob/master/README.md [md-security]: https://markdown-it-py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/other.html [md-performance]: https://markdown-it-py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/other.html [md-plugins]: https://markdown-it-py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/plugins.html

* class Core
*
* Top-level rules executor. Glues block/inline parsers and does intermediate

modules.name: markdown_it.presets.zero
documentation:
"Zero" preset, with nothing enabled. Useful for manual configuring of simple
modes. For example, to parse bold/italic only.

modules.name: markdown_it.presets.commonmark
documentation:
Commonmark default options.

This differs to presets.default,
primarily in that it allows HTML and does not enable components:

- block: table
- inline: strikethrough

classes.name: markdown_it.presets.gfm_like
documentation:
GitHub Flavoured Markdown (GFM) like.

This adds the linkify, table and strikethrough components to CommmonMark.

Note, it lacks task-list items and raw HTML filtering,
to meet the the full GFM specification
(see https://github.github.com/gfm/#autolinks-extension-).

functions.name: markdown_it.common.normalize_url.validateLink
documentation:
Validate URL link is allowed in output.

This validator can prohibit more than really needed to prevent XSS.
It's a tradeoff to keep code simple and to be secure by default.

Note: url should be normalized at this point, and existing entities decoded.

modules.name: markdown_it.rules_core.text_join
documentation:
Join raw text tokens with the rest of the text

This is set as a separate rule to provide an opportunity for plugins
to run text replacements after text join, but before escape join.

For example, `\:)` shouldn't be replaced with an emoji.

functions.name: markdown_it.rules_core.text_join.text_join
documentation: Join raw text for escape sequences (`text_special`) tokens with the rest of the text

modules.name: markdown_it.ruler
documentation:
class Ruler

Helper class, used by [[MarkdownIt#core]], [[MarkdownIt#block]] and
[[MarkdownIt#inline]] to manage sequences of functions (rules):

- keep rules in defined order
- assign the name to each rule
- enable/disable rules
- add/replace rules
- allow assign rules to additional named chains (in the same)
- caching lists of active rules

You will not need use this class directly until write plugins. For simple
rules control use [[MarkdownIt.disable]], [[MarkdownIt.enable]] and
[[MarkdownIt.use]].

variables.name: markdown_it.ruler.RuleFuncTv
documentation: A rule function, whose signature is dependent on the state type.

methods.name: markdown_it.ruler.Ruler.__find__
documentation: Find rule index by name

methods.name: markdown_it.ruler.Ruler.__compile__
documentation: Build rules lookup cache

methods.name: markdown_it.ruler.Ruler.at
documentation:
Replace rule by name with new function & options.

:param ruleName: rule name to replace.
:param fn: new rule function.
:param options: new rule options (not mandatory).
:raises: KeyError if name not found

methods.name: markdown_it.ruler.Ruler.before
documentation:
Add new rule to chain before one with given name.

:param beforeName: new rule will be added before this one.
:param ruleName: new rule will be added before this one.
:param fn: new rule function.
:param options: new rule options (not mandatory).
:raises: KeyError if name not found

methods.name: markdown_it.ruler.Ruler.after
documentation:
Add new rule to chain after one with given name.

:param afterName: new rule will be added after this one.
:param ruleName: new rule will be added after this one.
:param fn: new rule function.
:param options: new rule options (not mandatory).
:raises: KeyError if name not found

methods.name: markdown_it.ruler.Ruler.push
documentation:
Push new rule to the end of chain.

:param ruleName: new rule will be added to the end of chain.
:param fn: new rule function.
:param options: new rule options (not mandatory).

methods.name: markdown_it.ruler.Ruler.enable
documentation:
Enable rules with given names.

:param names: name or list of rule names to enable.
:param ignoreInvalid: ignore errors when rule not found
:raises: KeyError if name not found and not ignoreInvalid
:return: list of found rule names

methods.name: markdown_it.ruler.Ruler.enableOnly
documentation:
Enable rules with given names, and disable everything else.

:param names: name or list of rule names to enable.
:param ignoreInvalid: ignore errors when rule not found
:raises: KeyError if name not found and not ignoreInvalid
:return: list of found rule names

methods.name: markdown_it.ruler.Ruler.disable
documentation:
Disable rules with given names.

:param names: name or list of rule names to enable.
:param ignoreInvalid: ignore errors when rule not found
:raises: KeyError if name not found and not ignoreInvalid
:return: list of found rule names

methods.name: markdown_it.ruler.Ruler.getRules
documentation:
Return array of active functions (rules) for given chain name.
It analyzes rules configuration, compiles caches if not exists and returns result.

Default chain name is `''` (empty string). It can't be skipped.
That's done intentionally, to keep signature monomorphic for high speed.

methods.name: markdown_it.ruler.Ruler.get_all_rules
documentation: Return all available rule names.

methods.name: markdown_it.ruler.Ruler.get_active_rules
documentation: Return the active rule names.

documentation: Process escaped chars and hardbreaks.

functions.name: markdown_it.rules_inline.fragments_join.fragments_join
documentation:
Clean up tokens after emphasis and strikethrough postprocessing:
merge adjacent text nodes into one and re-calculate all token levels

This is necessary because initially emphasis delimiter markers (`*, _, ~`)
are treated as their own separate text tokens. Then emphasis rule either
leaves them as text (needed to merge with adjacent text) or turns them
into opening/closing tags (which messes up levels inside).

modules.name: markdown_it.common.utils
documentation: Utilities for parsing source text

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.charCodeAt
documentation:
Returns the Unicode value of the character at the specified location.

@param - index The zero-based index of the desired character.
If there is no character at the specified index, NaN is returned.

This was added for compatibility with python

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.charStrAt
documentation:
Returns the Unicode value of the character at the specified location.

@param - index The zero-based index of the desired character.
If there is no character at the specified index, NaN is returned.

This was added for compatibility with python

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.arrayReplaceAt
documentation:
Remove element from array and put another array at those position.
Useful for some operations with tokens

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.fromCodePoint
documentation:
Convert ordinal to unicode.

Note, in the original Javascript two string characters were required,
for codepoints larger than `0xFFFF`.
But Python 3 can represent any unicode codepoint in one character.

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.replaceEntityPattern
documentation:
Convert HTML entity patterns,
see https://spec.commonmark.org/0.30/#entity-references

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.stripEscape
documentation: Strip escape \ characters

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.escapeHtml
documentation: Replace special characters "&", "<", ">" and '"' to HTML-safe sequences.

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.isSpace
documentation: Check if character code is a whitespace.

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.isStrSpace
documentation: Check if character is a whitespace.

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.isWhiteSpace
documentation: Zs (unicode class) || [\t\f\v\r\n]

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.isPunctChar
documentation: Check if character is a punctuation character.
Currently without astral characters support.

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.isMdAsciiPunct
documentation:
Markdown ASCII punctuation characters.

```
!, ", #, $, %, &, ', (, ), *, +, ,, -, ., /, :, ;, <, =, >, ?, @, [, \, ], ^, _, `, {, |, }, or ~
```

See <http://spec.commonmark.org/0.15/#ascii-punctuation-character>

Don't confuse with unicode punctuation !!! It lacks some chars in ascii range.

functions.name: markdown_it.common.utils.normalizeReference
documentation: Helper to unify [reference labels].

modules.name: markdown_it.renderer
documentation:
class Renderer

Generates HTML from parsed token stream. Each instance has independent
copy of rules. Those can be rewritten with ease. Also, you can add new
rules if you create plugin and adds new token types.

classes.name: markdown_it.renderer.RendererHTML

methods.name: markdown_it.renderer.RendererHTML.render
documentation:
Takes token stream and generates HTML.

:param tokens: list on block tokens to render
:param options: params of parser instance
:param env: additional data from parsed input

methods.name: markdown_it.renderer.RendererHTML.renderInline
documentation:
The same as `render`, but for single token of `inline` type.

```
param tokens

list on block tokens to render

param options

params of parser instance

param env

additional data from parsed input (references, for example)
```

methods.name: markdown_it.renderer.RendererHTML.renderToken
documentation:
Default token renderer.

Can be overridden by custom function

:param idx: token index to render
:param options: params of parser instance

methods.name: markdown_it.renderer.RendererHTML.renderAttrs
documentation: Render token attributes to string.

methods.name: markdown_it.renderer.RendererHTML.renderInlineAsText
documentation:
Special kludge for image `alt` attributes to conform CommonMark spec.

Don't try to use it! Spec requires to show `alt` content with stripped markup,
instead of simple escaping.

:param tokens: list on block tokens to render
:param options: params of parser instance
:param env: additional data from parsed input

Contains render rules for tokens. Can be updated and extended. Example: Each rule is called as independent static function with fixed signature: ``` class Renderer: def token_type_name(self, tokens, idx, options, env) { # ... return renderedHTML ``` ``` class CustomRenderer(RendererHTML): def strong_open(self, tokens, idx, options, env): return '' def strong_close(self, tokens, idx, options, env): return '' md = MarkdownIt(renderer_cls=CustomRenderer) result = md.render(...) ``` See for more details and examples.

methods.name: markdown_it.rules_inline.state_inline.StateInline.push
documentation:
Push new token to "stream".
If pending text exists - flush it as text token

methods.name: markdown_it.rules_inline.state_inline.StateInline.scanDelims
documentation:
Scan a sequence of emphasis-like markers, and determine whether
it can start an emphasis sequence or end an emphasis sequence.

 - start - position to scan from (it should point at a valid marker);
 - canSplitWord - determine if these markers can be found inside a word

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.__init__
documentation:
Main parser class

```
param config

name of configuration to load or a pre-defined dictionary

param options_update

dictionary that will be merged into config["options"]

param renderer_cls

the class to load as the renderer:
``self.renderer = renderer_cls(self)

source:5: (WARNING/2) Inline literal start-string without end-string.
```

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.set
documentation:
Set parser options (in the same format as in constructor).
Probably, you will never need it, but you can change options after constructor call.

__Note:__ To achieve the best possible performance, don't modify a
`markdown-it` instance options on the fly. If you need multiple configurations
it's best to create multiple instances and initialize each with separate config.

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.configure
documentation:
Batch load of all options and component settings.
This is an internal method, and you probably will not need it.
But if you will - see available presets and data structure
[here](https://github.com/markdown-it/markdown-it/tree/master/lib/presets)

We strongly recommend to use presets instead of direct config loads.
That will give better compatibility with next versions.

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.get_all_rules
documentation: Return the names of all active rules.

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.get_active_rules
documentation: Return the names of all active rules.

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.enable
documentation:
Enable list or rules. (chainable)

```
param names

rule name or list of rule names to enable.

param ignoreInvalid

set  to ignore errors when rule not found.
```

It will automatically find appropriate components,
containing rules with given names. If rule not found, and `ignoreInvalid`
not set - throws exception.

Example:

```
md = MarkdownIt().enable(['sub', 'sup']).disable('smartquotes')
```

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.disable
documentation:
The same as [[MarkdownIt.enable]], but turn specified rules off. (chainable)

:param names: rule name or list of rule names to disable.
:param ignoreInvalid: set `true` to ignore errors when rule not found.

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.reset_rules
documentation: A context manager, that will reset the current enabled rules on exit.

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.add_render_rule
documentation:
Add a rule for rendering a particular Token type.

Only applied when ``renderer.__output__ == fmt``

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.use
documentation:
Load specified plugin with given params into current parser instance. (chainable)

It's just a sugar to call `plugin(md, params)` with curring.

Example:

```
def func(tokens, idx):
    tokens[idx].content = tokens[idx].content.replace('foo', 'bar')
md = MarkdownIt().use(plugin, 'foo_replace', 'text', func)
```

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.parse
documentation:
Parse the source string to a token stream

:param src: source string
:param env: environment sandbox

Parse input string and return list of block tokens (special token type
"inline" will contain list of inline tokens).

`env` is used to pass data between "distributed" rules and return additional
metadata like reference info, needed for the renderer. It also can be used to
inject data in specific cases. Usually, you will be ok to pass `{}`,
and then pass updated object to renderer.

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.render
documentation:
Render markdown string into html. It does all magic for you :).

:param src: source string
:param env: environment sandbox
:returns: The output of the loaded renderer

`env` can be used to inject additional metadata (`{}` by default).
But you will not need it with high probability. See also comment
in [[MarkdownIt.parse]].

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.parseInline
documentation:
The same as [[MarkdownIt.parse]] but skip all block rules.

:param src: source string
:param env: environment sandbox

It returns the
block tokens list with the single `inline` element, containing parsed inline
tokens in `children` property. Also updates `env` object.

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.renderInline
documentation:
Similar to [[MarkdownIt.render]] but for single paragraph content. :param src: source string :param env: environment sandbox Similar to [[MarkdownIt.render]] but for single paragraph content. Result will NOT be wrapped into `

` tags.

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.validateLink
documentation:
Validate if the URL link is allowed in output.

This validator can prohibit more than really needed to prevent XSS.
It's a tradeoff to keep code simple and to be secure by default.

Note: the url should be normalized at this point, and existing entities decoded.
link methods

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.normalizeLink
documentation:
Normalize destination URLs in links

```
[label]:   destination   'title'
        ^^^^^^^^^^^
```

methods.name: markdown_it.main.MarkdownIt.normalizeLinkText
documentation:
Normalize autolink content

```
<destination>
~~~~~~~~~~~
```

variables.name: markdown_it.utils.EnvType
documentation:
Type for the environment sandbox used in parsing and rendering,
which stores mutable variables for use by plugins and rules.
note: could use TypeAlias in python 3.10

A dictionary, with attribute access to core markdownit configuration options.

functions.name: markdown_it.token.convert_attrs
documentation:
Convert Token.attrs set as `None` or `[[key, value], ...]` to a dict.

This improves compatibility with upstream markdown-it.

methods.name: markdown_it.token.Token.attrItems
documentation: Get (key, value) list of attrs.

methods.name: markdown_it.token.Token.attrPush
documentation: Add `[ name, value ]` attribute to list. Init attrs if necessary.

methods.name: markdown_it.token.Token.attrSet
documentation: Set `name` attribute to `value`. Override old value if exists.

methods.name: markdown_it.token.Token.attrGet
documentation: Get the value of attribute `name`, or null if it does not exist.

methods.name: markdown_it.token.Token.attrJoin
documentation:
Join value to existing attribute via space.
Or create new attribute if not exists.
Useful to operate with token classes.

methods.name: markdown_it.token.Token.copy
documentation: Return a shallow copy of the instance.

methods.name: markdown_it.token.Token.as_dict
documentation:
Return the token as a dictionary.

```
param children

Also convert children to dicts

param as_upstream

Ensure the output dictionary is equal to that created by markdown-it
For example, attrs are converted to null or lists

param meta_serializer

hook for serializing Token.meta

param filter

A callable whose return code determines whether an
attribute or element is included (True) or dropped (False).
Is called with the (key, value) pair.

param dict_factory

A callable to produce dictionaries from.
For example, to produce ordered dictionaries instead of normal Python
dictionaries, pass in collections.OrderedDict.
```

methods.name: markdown_it.token.Token.from_dict
documentation: Convert a dict to a Token.

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.type
documentation: Type of the token (string, e.g. "paragraph_open")

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.tag
documentation: HTML tag name, e.g. 'p'

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.nesting
documentation:
Level change (number in {-1, 0, 1} set), where:
-  `1` means the tag is opening
-  `0` means the tag is self-closing
- `-1` means the tag is closing

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.attrs
documentation:
HTML attributes.
Note this differs from the upstream "list of lists" format,
although than an instance can still be initialised with this format.

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.map
documentation: Source map info. Format: `[ line_begin, line_end ]`

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.level
documentation: Nesting level, the same as `state.level`

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.children
documentation: Array of child nodes (inline and img tokens).

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.content
documentation: Inner content, in the case of a self-closing tag (code, html, fence, etc.),

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.markup
documentation: '*' or '_' for emphasis, fence string for fence, etc.

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.info
documentation:
Additional information:
- Info string for "fence" tokens
- The value "auto" for autolink "link_open" and "link_close" tokens
- The string value of the item marker for ordered-list "list_item_open" tokens

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.meta
documentation: A place for plugins to store any arbitrary data

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.block
documentation:
True for block-level tokens, false for inline tokens.
Used in renderer to calculate line breaks

class_variables.name: markdown_it.token.Token.hidden
documentation:
If true, ignore this element when rendering.
Used for tight lists to hide paragraphs.

modules.name: markdown_it.tree
documentation:
A tree representation of a linear markdown-it token stream.

This module is not part of upstream JavaScript markdown-it.

functions.name: markdown_it.tree._removesuffix
documentation:
Remove a suffix from a string.

Replace this with str.removesuffix() from stdlib when minimum Python
version is 3.9.

classes.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a `SyntaxTreeNode` from a token stream.

If `create_root` is True, create a root node for the document.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.to_tokens
documentation: Recover the linear token stream.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.is_root
documentation: Is the node a special root node?

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.is_nested
documentation:
Is this node nested?.

Returns `True` if the node represents a `Token` pair and tokens in the
sequence between them, where `Token.nesting` of the first `Token` in
the pair is 1 and nesting of the other `Token` is -1.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.siblings
documentation:
Get siblings of the node.

Gets the whole group of siblings, including self.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.type
documentation:
Get a string type of the represented syntax.

- "root" for root nodes
- `Token.type` if the node represents an unnested token
- `Token.type` of the opening token, with "_open" suffix stripped, if
    the node represents a nester token pair

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.next_sibling
documentation:
Get the next node in the sequence of siblings.

Returns `None` if this is the last sibling.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.previous_sibling
documentation:
Get the previous node in the sequence of siblings.

Returns `None` if this is the first sibling.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode._add_child
documentation: Make a child node for `self`.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode._set_children_from_tokens
documentation:
Convert the token stream to a tree structure and set the resulting
nodes as children of `self`.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.pretty
documentation: Create an XML style string of the tree.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.walk
documentation:
Recursively yield all descendant nodes in the tree starting at self.

The order mimics the order of the underlying linear token
stream (i.e. depth first).

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode._attribute_token
documentation:
Return the `Token` that is used as the data source for the
properties defined below.
NOTE:
The values of the properties defined below directly map to properties
of the underlying `Token`s. A root node does not translate to a `Token`
object, so calling these property getters on a root node will raise an
`AttributeError`.
There is no mapping for `Token.nesting` because the `is_nested` property
provides that data, and can be called on any node type, including root.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.tag
documentation: html tag name, e.g. "p"

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.attrs
documentation: Html attributes.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.attrGet
documentation: Get the value of attribute `name`, or null if it does not exist.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.map
documentation: Source map info. Format: `tuple[ line_begin, line_end ]`

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.level
documentation: nesting level, the same as `state.level`

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.content
documentation:
In a case of self-closing tag (code, html, fence, etc.), it
has contents of this tag.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.markup
documentation: '*' or '_' for emphasis, fence string for fence, etc.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.info
documentation: fence infostring

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.meta
documentation: A place for plugins to store an arbitrary data.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.block
documentation: True for block-level tokens, false for inline tokens.

methods.name: markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode.hidden
documentation:
If it's true, ignore this element when rendering.
Used for tight lists to hide paragraphs.

A Markdown syntax tree node.

A class that can be used to construct a tree representation of a linear
`markdown-it-py` token stream.

Each node in the tree represents either:
  - root of the Markdown document
  - a single unnested `Token`
  - a `Token` "_open" and "_close" token pair, and the tokens nested in
      between
